docs.title: MPaCT Website Docs
docs.text:
Maintenance documentation for the MPaCT public website at nano.nau.edu.

This site is for junior developers, the docs maintainer, and staff members who need to make small content edits with clear guardrails.

docs.title: Start Here
docs.text: Need Go To Update public website page content Page Guides Learn how this docs site is organized Getting Started Find guides by maintenance topic Tags

docs.title: Current Page Guides
docs.text:
• About Us
  • Contact Us
  • Degree Programs

Search-first workflow

Use search for exact selectors, ids, and filenames like ackText, gateway-card, submitBtn, stat-value, About_Us.html, or JS/script.js.

docs.title: What This Site Is Not
docs.text: This is not the MPaCT public website. It does not copy the public website mega menu, footer, or marketing layout. It uses MkDocs Material for the documentation experience and NAU/MPaCT branding for context.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Use this section to understand the maintenance model for the MPaCT public website and the MPaCT Docs Site.

docs.title: Maintenance Model
docs.text:
• The MPaCT public website is the user-facing site at nano.nau.edu.
  • The MPaCT Docs Site explains how to update and maintain that public website.
  • Existing page guides focus on exact files, selectors, page sections, and side effects.

docs.title: First Workflow
docs.text:
1. Search for the public page or selector you need.
  2. Open the matching page guide.
  3. Read the side effects before editing.
  4. Make the smallest safe change.
  5. Verify the public page behavior in a browser.

docs.title: Page Guides
docs.text: Page guides explain how to maintain specific pages on the MPaCT public website.

docs.title: Available Guides
docs.text:
Public Page Guide Main Maintenance Areas About_Us.html About Us Stats, pipeline, affiliations, acknowledgement text, CTA buttons Contact_Us.html Contact Us Gateway cards, dynamic form labels, submit and cancel buttons degree-programs.html Degree Programs Degree rows, hero stats, metrology skills, campus cards, CTA links

Guide format

Each page guide should name the target file, list searchable selectors, show exact snippets, and call out side effects.

docs.title: About Us Page Guide
docs.text:
Target file: About_Us.html

How to update stats, vision pipeline, affiliation cards, the publication acknowledgement, and CTA buttons.

docs.title: Content Management Reference
docs.text: What You're Changing Search For (Ctrl+F) Technical Name Stat number class="stat-value" .stat-value div Stat label class="stat-title" .stat-title div Pipeline stage title class="pipeline-stage .pipeline-stage div Stage number badge class="stage-number" .stage-number div Stage tag link class="stage-tag-link" a.stage-tag-link span Affiliation card class="affiliation-card .affiliation-card div Affiliation area item class="affiliation-areas" .affiliation-areas li Ack text id="ackText" #ackText p CTA buttons class="about-cta__actions" .about-cta__actions div

docs.title: Edit a Stat Number or Label
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="stats-strip">

<div class="stat-item about-reveal about-delay-1">
  <div class="stat-value">$13<span class="stat-suffix">M</span></div>
  <div class="stat-title">Facility Investment</div>
</div>

<!-- stat-suffix renders the unit in smaller lighter text -->
<!-- Remove the <span> for a plain number with no suffix -->

Note

The about-delay-1 through about-delay-4 classes stagger the animation. Keep them in order when adding a new stat or the reveal sequence will be uneven.

docs.title: Add a Stat
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="stats-strip">

<!-- Paste before the closing </div> of stats-strip -->
<div class="stat-item about-reveal about-delay-5">
  <div class="stat-value">10<span class="stat-suffix">+</span></div>
  <div class="stat-title">Industry Partners</div>
</div>

Side Effect

The stats-strip uses flex layout with wrap. Five stats on a narrow screen (under 900px) will wrap to a second row instead of staying in one line. Test at 768px after adding.

docs.title: Edit a Pipeline Stage Title or Description
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="vision-pipeline">

<div class="pipeline-stage about-reveal about-delay-1">
  <div class="stage-icon"><i class="fas fa-flask"></i></div>
  <div class="stage-card">
    <div class="stage-number">01</div>
    <h3>Research Infrastructure</h3>  <!-- stage heading -->
    <p>Description text here...</p>  <!-- body paragraph -->
    <div class="stage-tags">
      <!-- tag links below -->
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

Note

The stage-number badge (01, 02, 03) is plain text - it does not auto-increment. If you reorder stages, update the numbers manually.

docs.title: Edit a Stage Tag Link
docs.text:
Stage tags are anchor links to other pages. Change both the href and the visible text.

Search for: class="stage-tag-link"

<a href="Equipment.html" class="stage-tag-link"><span>AFM & SEM</span></a>

<!-- Change href and the text inside <span> -->
<a href="services.html" class="stage-tag-link"><span>Contract Testing</span></a>

docs.title: Change a Stage Icon Color
docs.text:
The stage-icon div accepts a color modifier class. Three are defined: (default blue), gold, green.

Search for: class="stage-icon"

<!-- Default (blue) -->
<div class="stage-icon"><i class="fas fa-flask"></i></div>

<!-- Gold -->
<div class="stage-icon gold"><i class="fas fa-graduation-cap"></i></div>

<!-- Green -->
<div class="stage-icon green"><i class="fas fa-handshake"></i></div>

docs.title: Edit an Affiliation Card
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="affiliations-grid">

<div class="affiliation-card about-reveal about-delay-1">
  <div class="affiliation-icon">
    <i class="fas fa-microchip"></i>   <!-- Font Awesome icon -->
  </div>
  <h3>College of Engineering, Informatics & Applied Sciences</h3>
  <ul class="affiliation-areas">
    <li>Semiconductor device fabrication</li>
    <li>MEMS & sensor prototyping</li>
    <li>Thin film process development</li>
  </ul>
</div>

Note

Icon color classes available on affiliation-icon: (default blue), gold, green, purple. Match these to the card's thematic color if adding a new one.

docs.title: Add a Research Area to an Existing Card
docs.text:
Search for: class="affiliation-areas"

<ul class="affiliation-areas">
  <li>Semiconductor device fabrication</li>
  <li>MEMS & sensor prototyping</li>
  <!-- Add new area: -->
  <li>Photonic device research</li>
</ul>

docs.title: Add a New Affiliation Card
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="affiliations-grid">

<!-- Paste inside affiliations-grid -->
<div class="affiliation-card about-reveal about-delay-5">
  <div class="affiliation-icon purple">
    <i class="fas fa-satellite-dish"></i>
  </div>
  <h3>Department Name</h3>
  <ul class="affiliation-areas">
    <li>Research area 1</li>
    <li>Research area 2</li>
  </ul>
</div>

Side Effect

The affiliations grid is a 4-column CSS grid. A 5th card wraps to a second row. If you want a 5-card layout to stay in one row, the grid-template-columns value in style.css must be updated. Test at 1280px.

docs.title: Update the Acknowledgement Wording
docs.text:
Search for: id="ackText"

<p id="ackText">"This work was performed in part at the Microelectronics
  Processing and Characterization Testing Lab (MPaCT) at Northern Arizona
  University, Flagstaff, AZ."
</p>

<!-- Edit only the text inside the <p> tag -->
<!-- The Copy button on the page reads this element's text automatically -->

Note

The Copy button is wired to getElementById("ackText") in JS/about.js. As long as the id stays as ackText, the button will copy whatever text you put inside this paragraph.

docs.title: Change Button Text or Destination
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="about-cta__actions">

<div class="about-cta__actions">
  <a href="Contact_Us.html?category=other" class="btn btn-primary">
    Request Lab Access
  </a>
  <a href="Equipment.html" class="btn btn-outline about-cta__outline">
    Browse Equipment
  </a>
</div>

docs.title: Contact Page Guide
docs.text:
Target file: Contact_Us.html

How to add, edit, and remove inquiry gateway cards and update form field labels and button text.

docs.title: Content Management Reference
docs.text: What You're Changing Search For (Ctrl+F) Technical Name Gateway card class="gateway-card" .gateway-card div Card icon class="fas fa-microscope" <i> inside gateway-card Card title class="gateway-title" .gateway-title h3 Card description class="gateway-desc" .gateway-desc p Page heading HOW CAN WE HELP YOU h1 in section.section-padding Form field label <!-- Common Fields --> label inside #contactForm Form field placeholder placeholder= input[placeholder] Submit button text id="submitBtn" #submitBtn button Cancel button text onclick="resetSelection()" button.btn-outline-gray

docs.title: 1. Inquiry Gateway Cards
docs.text: The gateway grid displays inquiry category cards. Each card, when clicked, calls selectCategory() which triggers the form below. The category string in onclick must match what the JavaScript routing in JS/script.js expects.

docs.title: Edit a Card Icon, Title, or Description
docs.text:
Search for: id="gatewayGrid"

<div class="gateway-card" onclick="selectCategory('equipment', this)">
  <i class="fas fa-microscope"></i>      <!-- Font Awesome icon -->
  <h3 class="gateway-title">EQUIPMENT INQUIRY</h3>
  <p class="gateway-desc">Short description text.</p>
</div>

docs.title: Add a New Gateway Card
docs.text:
Add the HTML card below, then open JS/script.js and add matching routing logic for the new category string.

Search for: id="gatewayGrid"

<!-- Step 1: Add inside id="gatewayGrid" in Contact_Us.html -->
<div class="gateway-card" onclick="selectCategory('recruitment', this)">
  <i class="fas fa-user-tie"></i>
  <h3 class="gateway-title">Recruitment</h3>
  <p class="gateway-desc">Hiring inquiries and graduate student positions.</p>
</div>

<!-- Step 2: In JS/script.js, find the categoryConfig or selectCategory -->
<!-- function and add a case for "recruitment" with a form config object. -->

Side Effect

Adding a card without the matching JS routing entry means clicking the card will call selectCategory("recruitment") but the JS will not know how to build the form - it will likely show a blank or broken form panel. Both the HTML and JS/script.js must be updated together.

docs.title: Remove a Gateway Card
docs.text:
Search for: onclick="selectCategory('sales'

<!-- Delete this entire block to remove the Vendor/Sales card: -->
<div class="gateway-card" onclick="selectCategory('sales', this)">
  <i class="fas fa-truck-loading"></i>
  <h3 class="gateway-title">Vendor / Sales</h3>
  <p class="gateway-desc">...</p>
</div>

Side Effect

The gateway grid is a 4-column grid (grid-4). Removing one card leaves 7 cards in a 4-3 layout - the last row will have only 3 cards, left-aligned. This is visually acceptable but if you want the grid to stay balanced, remove cards in pairs or update the grid class to grid-3 in Contact_Us.html to get a 3-column layout.

docs.title: 2. Common Form Field Labels and Placeholders
Edit a Label or Placeholder
docs.text:
Search for: <!-- Common Fields -->

<!-- Each field block looks like this: -->
<div>
  <label class="block font-bold mb-1 required">First Name</label>
  <input type="text" name="first_name" class="form-control"
         placeholder="Alex" required maxlength="25" data-name-field>
</div>

<!-- Edit the label text and/or the placeholder attribute -->

Note

The required class on the <label> adds a visual asterisk via CSS. Remove it from the label if a field is becoming optional, and also remove the required attribute from the <input> - both must change together.

docs.title: Make a Field Optional (Remove Required)
docs.text:
Search for: name="phone"

<!-- Phone is already optional - this is the pattern to follow: -->
<label class="block font-bold mb-1">Phone</label>  <!-- no "required" class -->
<input type="tel" name="phone" class="form-control"
       placeholder="(555) 123-4567" inputmode="numeric"
       maxlength="14" data-phone-field>  <!-- no "required" attribute -->

Note

Server-side validation in includes/validation.php mirrors client-side validation. If you remove a required attribute from a field, also check validation.php to make sure the server does not still reject submissions that omit that field.

docs.title: Edit the Page Heading
docs.text:
Search for: HOW CAN WE HELP YOU

<h1 class="text-blue mb-10">HOW CAN WE HELP YOU?</h1>
<p class="text-muted page-subtitle">
  Select a category below to route your inquiry to the correct department.
</p>

docs.title: Change the Submit Button Label
docs.text:
Search for: id="submitBtn"

<button type="submit" id="submitBtn" class="btn btn-primary">
  Submit Request
</button>

<!-- Change only the visible text between the tags -->

Note

JS/script.js disables #submitBtn while a form is submitting to prevent duplicate submissions. Do not change the id="submitBtn" - the JS depends on it.

docs.title: Change the Cancel Button Label
docs.text:
Search for: onclick="resetSelection()"

<button type="button" onclick="resetSelection()"
        class="btn btn-outline btn-outline-gray">
  Cancel
</button>

docs.title: Degree Programs Page Guide
docs.text:
Target file: degree-programs.html

How to add, edit, and remove degree program entries, hero stats, campus cards, and CTA links.

docs.title: Content Management Reference
docs.text: What You're Changing Search For (Ctrl+F) Technical Name Degree program card <article class="program-row .program-row article Program title class="program-row-title" .program-row-title h3 Degree badge class="program-row-degree" .program-row-degree span Description text class="program-row-desc" .program-row-desc p Feature tag class="program-row-feature" .program-row-feature div View Degree link class="program-row-link" a.program-row-link Hero stat number class="ml-pstat__num" .ml-pstat__num Hero stat label class="ml-pstat__label" .ml-pstat__label Hero tag class="ml-hero__panel-tag" .ml-hero__panel-tag span Metrology skill class="metrology-skill" .metrology-skill div Campus card class="campus-card" .campus-card div CTA buttons class="cta-buttons" .cta-buttons div

docs.title: Add a New Degree Program
docs.text:
Each program is an <article> element inside the program-rows div. Copy one complete article block (from <article> to </article>) and paste it at the end, before the closing </div> of program-rows.

Search for: <div class="program-rows">

<!-- Paste before the closing </div> of program-rows -->
<article class="program-row reveal-on-scroll" id="your-program-id">
  <div class="program-row-image">
    <img src="Images/your-image.jpg" alt="Program description">
  </div>
  <div class="program-row-content">
    <div class="program-row-header">
      <h3 class="program-row-title">Program Name</h3>
      <span class="program-row-degree">Bachelor of Science</span>
    </div>
    <p class="program-row-desc">Description of the program...</p>
    <div class="program-row-features">
      <div class="program-row-feature">
        <!-- SVG icon goes here (copy from any existing feature) -->
        Feature Label
      </div>
      <!-- Add more features as needed -->
    </div>
    <a href="https://degree-search.nau.edu/..." target="_blank"
       class="program-row-link">
      View Degree Details
      <!-- Arrow SVG (copy from any existing program-row-link) -->
    </a>
  </div>
</article>

Note

The id attribute on the article (e.g. id="computer-engineering") is used by internal anchor links from other pages. Give it a short, hyphenated id that matches the program name.

docs.title: Edit a Program Title, Description, or Degree Badge
docs.text:
Search for: class="program-row-title"

<h3 class="program-row-title">Computer Engineering</h3>
<span class="program-row-degree">Bachelor of Science</span>
<p class="program-row-desc">Edit description text here...</p>

docs.title: Edit a Feature Tag Inside a Program
docs.text:
Each program has up to 4 feature items. Each has an SVG icon and a text label. To change the label, edit the text after the closing </svg> tag.

Search for: class="program-row-feature"

<div class="program-row-feature">
  <svg ...>...</svg>
  Computer Architecture    <!-- Edit this text -->
</div>

Note

The SVG icons are inline. If you want a different icon, copy one from another feature in the file or use a different inline SVG path. Font Awesome is NOT used for these icons.

docs.title: Update the "View Degree Details" Link
docs.text:
Search for: class="program-row-link"

<a href="https://degree-search.nau.edu/degree/computer-engineering-bs"
   target="_blank" class="program-row-link">
  View Degree Details
</a>

<!-- Change href to the new NAU degree search URL -->

docs.title: Temporarily Hide a Program
docs.text:
Search for: id="electrical" (or the id of the program)

<!-- Comment out the entire article: -->
<!--
<article class="program-row reveal-on-scroll" id="electrical">
  ...
</article>
-->

Side Effect

Program rows are block-level elements stacked vertically. Hiding one closes the gap cleanly - adjacent programs will move up. No CSS adjustment needed.

docs.title: Edit a Stat Number or Label
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="ml-hero__panel-stats">

<div class="ml-pstat__num">6</div>        <!-- the number -->
<div class="ml-pstat__label">Degree Tracks</div>  <!-- the label -->

<!-- With a suffix in lighter weight: -->
<div class="ml-pstat__num">100<em>%</em></div>

Note

The <em> tag renders the suffix character in a lighter style. Keep it for symbols like %, +, K. Remove for a plain number.

docs.title: Add or Remove a Hero Tag
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="ml-hero__panel-tags">

<div class="ml-hero__panel-tags">
  <span class="ml-hero__panel-tag">Hands-on Research</span>
  <span class="ml-hero__panel-tag">Advanced Facilities</span>
  <!-- Add a new tag: -->
  <span class="ml-hero__panel-tag">Industry Mentors</span>
</div>

docs.title: Add or Remove a Skill
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="metrology-skills">

<div class="metrology-skills">
  <div class="metrology-skill">
    <svg ...>...</svg>   <!-- checkmark icon - copy from a sibling -->
    <span>Optical and Electron Microscopy</span>
  </div>

  <!-- Add a new skill by copying one block above: -->
  <div class="metrology-skill">
    <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="20" height="20" fill="var(--nau-green)">
      <path d="M9 16.17L4.83 12l-1.42 1.41L9 19 21 7l-1.41-1.41z" />
    </svg>
    <span>Thin Film Thickness Measurement</span>
  </div>
</div>

docs.title: Edit Campus Name, Description, or Badge
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="campus-grid">

<div class="campus-card reveal-on-scroll">
  <div class="campus-card-image">
    <img src="Images/flagstaff_campus.jpg" alt="Flagstaff Campus">
    <div class="campus-card-badge">Main Campus</div>  <!-- badge text -->
  </div>
  <div class="campus-card-body">
    <h3>Flagstaff Campus</h3>           <!-- campus name -->
    <p>Description text here...</p>     <!-- paragraph -->
  </div>
</div>

Note

The second campus card uses campus-card-badge-gold for a gold badge variant. Keep that class if you want the gold color, or remove it for the default blue.

docs.title: Change Button Text or Destination URL
docs.text:
Search for: <div class="cta-buttons">

<div class="cta-buttons">
  <a href="https://catalog.nau.edu/Catalog/" target="_blank"
     class="btn btn-secondary">NAU Academic Catalog</a>
  <a href="https://nau.edu/college-engineering/" target="_blank"
     class="btn btn-primary">College of Engineering</a>
</div>

docs.title: Reference
docs.text: Reusable conventions for writing and maintaining MPaCT Docs content.

docs.title: Reference Pages
docs.text: • Tags

docs.title: Tags
docs.text: Use tags lightly. Tags support discovery across page guides and references, but they do not replace navigation.

docs.title: Starting Tag Set
docs.text: Tag Use For HTML Target HTML files and markup snippets CSS Selectors, visual classes, and style side effects JavaScript Script dependencies, dynamic behavior, and ids read by JS Forms Form fields, validation, submission, and category routing Navigation Header, footer, menu, active states, and links Equipment Equipment catalog, reservation, status, and instrument content Content Edit Small text, card, CTA, stat, and label changes Reference Reusable docs conventions
